what_it_does: Arbitrates a substrate-unification proposal (the UNION MAP) against three adversarial challenges, re-verifies selected claims against the live repo, corrects factual errors, and marks which parts of the union are earned, inferred, or blocked.
observations: Every load-bearing claim is tagged with an evidence status: Observed, Inferred, or Verified.

Corrects the map on multiple counts: agent_sessions is 1,068 files not 13; VerdictPanelRegistry is actually PanelRegistry; a second CapabilityRegistry exists; dna/types.json and substrate-assemble.py live in a separate repo, not /home/tim/company.
Identifies a nested address grammar vec://cluster://...#space=scale:... that a flat scheme-row table cannot parse.
Distinguishes three independent layers: address syntax, resolution mechanism, and identity model.
Finds two resolvers in the codebase: resolve_address and resolve_scope.
RULE_OPS is a closed predicate grammar with no producer op, so lifters.extract cannot be folded into the same recogniser model as triggers/forms.
Recommends local Postgres+pgvector as the storage target and cloud Supabase as a later sync/realtime tier, not the migration target.
Reports a storage seam ratio of ~16% (10 Protocol methods versus ~61 methods called on self.store).
Verifies that /api/projection and the MCP project door route through the same Suite.project engine.
Notes a real structured_output drop at _turn_done but labels the '3-line fix' as Inferred, not verified.
Flags Shape-B (events, marks, pins, dispositions, annotations, findings, chat, mail) as an event tier with no address scheme that the thesis must stop claiming to subsume.
standouts: The corrected one-sentence thesis: one address syntax is real, but four areas are decisions not bridges.
The nested vec://cluster://...#space=scale:... grammar as a concrete counter-example to flat scheme-row unification.
The local-first Postgres+pgvector storage recommendation with the 10/61 seam ratio as the first deliverable.
The structured_output drop verification and the RULE_OPS/lifter producer boundary.
conventions: Markdown section hierarchy with numbered parts and tables.
Code anchors given as path:line references.
Evidence-status labels applied to individual claims.
Adversarial arbitration structure: Map said / Challenge said / Verified truth.
Uses GO / BLOCKS / FEASIBLE verdict labels for build recommendations.
Ends with a single corrected summary sentence and a key-file anchor list.
concerns: Some load-bearing claims remain Inferred despite the mandate to label, e.g. the '3-line fix' for structured_output.
The document is provisional yet uses forceful arbitration language ('BLOCKS','decisions, not bridges') that could harden provisional findings.
Cross-repo DNA numbers are reported but marked as self-reported and uncited to live Company files.
The seam denominator (~61 self.store calls) is chosen over alternative counts and could be disputed.
It mixes synthesis, build planning, and risk register in one dense document.
notes: A self-aware arbitration of a unification proposal that labels every major claim by evidence status and draws a hard line between what can be built and what must be decided first.
questions: What are the exact filenames of the UNION MAP and the three adversarial challenges it answers?
Who is the intended consumer of this synthesis: the build plan, a triad review, or Tim?
How will the four 'decisions, not bridges' be resolved before building?
What is the precise scope of the structured_output fix?
Which specific files contain the static ui:// registry and the runtime /api/ui_info surface it must be reconciled with?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose as a provisional, honestly-statused arbitration; it also acts as a prescriptive build-plan and risk register, which goes slightly beyond pure synthesis.
apparent_themes: Evidence-status honesty as a first-class writing discipline.
Separating address syntax, resolution, and identity rather than flattening them.
Pushing back on over-abstraction by naming concrete counter-examples.
Local-first storage with cloud as a later sync layer.
Awareness of existing deduplication so consolidation work does not duplicate what already exists.
Cross-repo dependency caution: generalize, do not adopt a design-specific substrate.
intention_signals: To settle a debate between an optimistic unification map and its critics, correct the factual record, and produce a grounded build plan that avoids claiming unification where only a decision remains.
novelty: Explicit evidence-status labels on nearly every load-bearing claim and a 'decisions, not bridges' framing that pushes back on row-add unification optimism.
contribution: It contributes an evidence-labelled arbitration of substrate-unification claims, a corrected inventory of scheme/resolver/identity mismatches, and a set of build-seam criteria that a fused system would keep as decision boundaries.
summary_for_embedding: A research synthesis arbitrating a substrate-unification map against three challenges, correcting counts and citations, separating address syntax/resolution/identity, and marking feasible build seams versus unresolved decision points.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, evidence-status-labelled foundation for deciding what parts of a code/design substrate union can be built and what must be decided first.
